title: neoachmandra cunninghamii
intro: Neoachmandra cunninghamii, also known as the 'Sandpaper Bush', is a plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Australia, characterized by its unique, sandpapery leaves. The medicinal properties of Neoachmandra cunninghamii are attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits in reducing pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Neoachmandra cunninghamii can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by extracting the iridoid glycosides using solvents to produce a standardized extract.
